channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Do Large Language Models Possess a Theory of Mind? A Comparative Evaluation Using the Strange Stories Paradigm
item.description: arXiv:2603.18007v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The study explores whether current Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit Theory of Mind (ToM) capabilities -- specifically, the ability to infer others' beliefs, intentions, and emotions from text. Given that LLMs are trained on language data without s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, tool, model, LLM, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TherapyGym: Evaluating and Aligning Clinical Fidelity and Safety in Therapy Chatbots
item.description: arXiv:2603.18008v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for mental-health support; yet prevailing evaluation methods--fluency metrics, preference tests, and generic dialogue benchmarks--fail to capture the clinically critical dimensions of psychotherapy. W...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, release, large language model, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Confident Is the First Token? An Uncertainty-Calibrated Prompt Optimization Framework for Large Language Model Classification and Understanding
item.description: arXiv:2603.18009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the widespread adoption of large language models (LLMs) in natural language processing, prompt engineering and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) have become mainstream to enhance LLMs' performance on complex tasks. However, LLMs generate outpu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, prompt, large language model, few-shot, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Agentic Framework for Political Biography Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2603.18010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The production of large-scale political datasets typically demands extracting structured facts from vast piles of unstructured documents or web sources, a task that traditionally relies on expensive human experts and remains prohibitively difficult to...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, product, context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Controllable Evidence Selection in Retrieval-Augmented Question Answering via Deterministic Utility Gating
item.description: arXiv:2603.18011v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many modern AI question-answering systems convert text into vectors and retrieve the closest matches to a user question. While effective for topical similarity, similarity scores alone do not explain why some retrieved text can serve as evidence while...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, arxiv, retrieval, vector, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DynaRAG: Bridging Static and Dynamic Knowledge in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: arXiv:2603.18012v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present DynaRAG, a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) framework designed to handle both static and time-sensitive information needs through dynamic knowledge integration. Unlike traditional RAG pipelines that rely solely on static corpora, DynaRA...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, API, tool, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learned but Not Expressed: Capability-Expression Dissociation in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.18013v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate the capacity to reconstruct and trace learned content from their training data under specific elicitation conditions, yet this capability does not manifest in standard generation contexts. This empirical observ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, large language model, alignment, context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Real-Time Trustworthiness Scoring for LLM Structured Outputs and Data Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2603.18014v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Structured Outputs from current LLMs exhibit sporadic errors, hindering enterprise AI efforts from realizing their immense potential. We present CONSTRUCT, a method to score the trustworthiness of LLM Structured Outputs in real-time, such that lower-s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, model, LLM, reasoning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MineDraft: A Framework for Batch Parallel Speculative Decoding
item.description: arXiv:2603.18016v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates large language model inference by using a smaller draft model to propose draft tokens that are subsequently verified by a larger target model. However, the performance of standard SD is often limited by the strict...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, large language model, product, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DEAF: A Benchmark for Diagnostic Evaluation of Acoustic Faithfulness in Audio Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.18048v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent Audio Multimodal Large Language Models (Audio MLLMs) demonstrate impressive performance on speech benchmarks, yet it remains unclear whether these models genuinely process acoustic signals or rely on text-based semantic inference. To systematic...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, large language model, model, audio, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Continually self-improving AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.18073v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern language model-based AI systems are remarkably powerful, yet their capabilities remain fundamentally capped by their human creators in three key ways. First, although a model's weights can be updated via fine-tuning, acquiring new knowledge fro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, research, fine-tuning, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-Trait Subspace Steering to Reveal the Dark Side of Human-AI Interaction
item.description: arXiv:2603.18085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent incidents have highlighted alarming cases where human-AI interactions led to negative psychological outcomes, including mental health crises and even user harm. As LLMs serve as sources of guidance, emotional support, and even informal therapy,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, context, model, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TeachingCoach: A Fine-Tuned Scaffolding Chatbot for Instructional Guidance to Instructors
item.description: arXiv:2603.18189v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Higher education instructors often lack timely and pedagogically grounded support, as scalable instructional guidance remains limited and existing tools rely on generic chatbot advice or non-scalable teaching center human-human consultations. We prese...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, model, arxiv, study, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Computationally Efficient Learning of Artificial Intelligence System Reliability Considering Error Propagation
item.description: arXiv:2603.18201v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly prominent in emerging smart cities, yet their reliability remains a critical concern. These systems typically operate through a sequence of interconnected functional stages, where upstream errors m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, platform, ICL, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Retrieval-Augmented LLM Agents: Learning to Learn from Experience
item.description: arXiv:2603.18272v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While large language models (LLMs) have advanced the development of general-purpose agents, achieving robust generalization to unseen tasks remains a significant challenge. Current approaches typically rely on either fine-tuning or training-free memor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, arxiv, context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Frayed RoPE and Long Inputs: A Geometric Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2603.18017v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rotary Positional Embedding (RoPE) is a widely adopted technique for encoding position in language models, which, while effective, causes performance breakdown when input length exceeds training length. Prior analyses assert (rightly) that long inputs...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, attention, transformer, model, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Engineering Verifiable Modularity in Transformers via Per-Layer Supervision
item.description: arXiv:2603.18029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformers resist surgical control. Ablating an attention head identified as critical for capitalization produces minimal behavioral change because distributed redundancy compensates for damage. This Hydra effect renders interpretability illusory: w...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, API, experiment, attention, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: InfoMamba: An Attention-Free Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Model
item.description: arXiv:2603.18031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Balancing fine-grained local modeling with long-range dependency capture under computational constraints remains a central challenge in sequence modeling. While Transformers provide strong token mixing, they suffer from quadratic complexity, whereas M...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, experiment, attention, context, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Adapting Methods for Domain-Specific Japanese Small LMs: Scale, Architecture, and Quantization
item.description: arXiv:2603.18037v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents a systematic methodology for building domain-specific Japanese small language models using QLoRA fine-tuning. We address three core questions: optimal training scale, base-model selection, and architecture-aware quantization. Stage...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, product, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NANOZK: Layerwise Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Verifiable Large Language Model Inference
item.description: arXiv:2603.18046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When users query proprietary LLM APIs, they receive outputs with no cryptographic assurance that the claimed model was actually used. Service providers could substitute cheaper models, apply aggressive quantization, or return cached responses - all un...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, large language model, attention, transformer, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, prompt engineering, context, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, audio, CoT, reasoning, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, LLM, vector, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, prompt, LLM, vector, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Beyond Accuracy: An Explainability-Driven Analysis of Harmful Content Detection
item.description: arXiv:2603.18015v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although automated harmful content detection systems are frequently used to monitor online platforms, moderators and end users frequently cannot understand the logic underlying their predictions. While recent studies have focused on increasing classif...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, context, model, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Taming Epilepsy: Mean Field Control of Whole-Brain Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2603.18035v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Controlling the high-dimensional neural dynamics during epileptic seizures remains a significant challenge due to the nonlinear characteristics and complex connectivity of the brain. In this paper, we propose a novel framework, namely Graph-Regularize...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, arxiv, framework, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: Don't Vibe Code, Do Skele-Code: Interactive No-Code Notebooks for Subject Matter Experts to Build Lower-Cost Agentic Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2603.18122v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Skele-Code is a natural-language and graph-based interface for building workflows with AI agents, designed especially for less or non-technical users. It supports incremental, interactive notebook-style development, and each step is converted to code ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Differentiating Between Failures and Domain Shifts in Industrial Data Streams
item.description: arXiv:2603.18032v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Anomaly and failure detection methods are crucial in identifying deviations from normal system operational conditions, which allows for actions to be taken in advance, usually preventing more serious damages. Long-lasting deviations indicate failures,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, product, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MST-Direct: Matching via Sinkhorn Transport for Multivariate Geostatistical Simulation with Complex Non-Linear Dependencies
item.description: arXiv:2603.18036v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multivariate geostatistical simulation requires the faithful reproduction of complex non-linear dependencies among geological variables, including bimodal distributions, step functions, and heteroscedastic relationships. Traditional methods such as th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, product, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, step-by-step, tool, example, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.description: arXiv:2603.18104v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prevailing AI training infrastructure assumes reverse-mode automatic differentiation over IEEE-754 arithmetic. The memory overhead of training relative to inference, optimizer complexity, and structural degradation of geometric properties through trai...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, paper, arxiv, framework, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: Quotient Geometry and Persistence-Stable Metrics for Swarm Configurations
item.description: arXiv:2603.18041v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Swarm and constellation reconfiguration can be viewed as motion of an unordered point configuration in an ambient space. Here, we provide persistence-stable, symmetry-invariant geometric representations for comparing and monitoring multi-agent configu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, model, example, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
